tokens: Okay, I think the Cabin Repress is next.

transcript.speaker: Duke

transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Young

Use purge valve to depress PGA as req'd (sic)".

tokens: Now where do you see that one?
Okay, Suit Fan 2 and Suit Fan Delta...

transcript.speaker: Young

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Laughing) Why do we do that every time (we get ready to go out and come back in).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, it says "ECS caution and water SEP lights out".
Doff gloves, stow on comm panel."
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Long Pause) Must admit my finger's a little tired, though.

Let's look at the caution panel.

tokens: That OPS latch on Charlie's OPS may be hot to touch since that cover was up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Pause) Okay; It's cool as a cucumber.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: So much for a thermal analysis.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Laughing) It's about 72 degrees F on the top of that thing.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: It's hard working from absolute zero.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Water's open; "remove purge valves; stow in
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Turn around and I'll get yours.

tokens: Okay, "disconnect LM O2 hoses, red to blue and blue to red; PGA (diverter valve), horizontal.

transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Grunting) I go to Suit Flow in the (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're supposed to have stowed these in there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Wait, wait, wait...Oh, yeah, we did.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, the lunar dust smells like gunpowder.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Really, really a strong odor to it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Yeah, remember on hammering on rocks, fresh rock powder does have a strong odor.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I hope it's not the (LM) oxygen (supply) that has the peculiar odor.
But it goes away after a little while but...
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, you can bet this Moon has been hammered on.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: But it is really a strong smell.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pointing at the Prep & Post Card) Okay, we got this, we got that, (garbled)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: "Disconnect PLSS H2O from PGA; connect LM."
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That sounds like a good deal.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Eager to get cooling) Now that's just one step right there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The trouble is, yours is back under the rock box right now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Long Pause) Never seen anybody sneak as nice.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I don't think you got anything on that Beta-cloth one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's a problem you have to face with it (meaning the Contact Soil Sampler).
You know you don't set it down very hard.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Have you got yours hooked up yet?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, I gave us some water (by briefly closing the LCG Pump breaker).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Reading) "PLSS mode, 0, both; and Audio CB, Open."
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, did you get your pump Off (as per the bottom of Surface 5-1)?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: A to Receive; B to Off; Mode, ICS/PTT (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: My initial O2 recharge's complete and I got 95 percent.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Ed, how are you doing today?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We just had a great time, having fun as well as the work.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know, Ed, when we got up on top of that mountain and I'd been driving up it all the way, and I turned around and looked down, I thought, man, you've just nearly bit off more than you can chew here.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You've got a lot of hash and I couldn't read you very well.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That's his beard sticking in the mike.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Well, maybe you're starting late, but you'll catch up in 3 or 4 months with a beard.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Hey, Ed, how about a little news from you.
Anything going on down there that's interesting?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Is that news you're asking for, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll see if we can get you something.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're running down some news reports for you right now.
And we're not pushing, John, but we would like to stay fairly close to timeline so you can get plenty of sleep tonight.
You're going to have a hard day tomorrow.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Wait a minute, they said we had 2 hours extra tonight where we could just sit around and talk to each other.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, the boss (meaning the Flight Director Gene Kranz, who is just coming on duty with his White Team) just said he wasn't really sure he wanted to do all that talking tonight.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We were going to use it to sleep with.
Or at least take it easy on fixing things up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: John, I'm sorry; you're in the hash again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: He said we're gonna use it for sleeping, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Ed; my OPS source pressure is 6100 (psi) and John's is 5900.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Houston, we've finished the initial PLSS recharge.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Well, if you're in a position to where you want to listen, I've got a little sports news for you.
If you'd like to hold off, let me know when (you'd like to hear the news).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're just changing out the batteries and stuff for the PLSS (as per Surface 5-3).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Right now, we've got New York (Knicks professional basketball team) won 3 to 1 over the (Boston) Celtics in their series.
The (Los Angeles) Lakers have finished up theirs, 4 to 2 over Milwaukee (Bucks).
And are standing by to see the outcome of the rest of the New York and Celtics games.
And the (Houston) Astros (professional baseball team) have finished up 4 in a row today.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now that wasn't a kind question.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll run some more news down here before long.
(Pause) And when you get your battery management (which is the last paragraph on 5-3), I'll give you the new procedure, Charlie; just give me a call.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Would you give us a clue as to where you are in the checklist?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we just finished changing out my LiOH and we're stowing the PLSS O2 and the OPS is removed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Ed, we had one small...(In) looking over the gear here, we had one small casualty.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And I checked the comm when I noticed it.
When he came in, I checked the comm and he was still sounding the same to me.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, he sounds good to us.
Do you happen to know when it occurred?
Or when you first saw it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I think it was during the ingress.
We got behind and y'all reminded us of it once (at
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, so we're missing a couple inches of antenna.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Ed, we're ready for the battery management (as per the right-hand column of 5-3).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Charlie, I'll give you the overall plan here to put the Lunar Batt on the LMP Bus, take Batts 1 and 2, Off, and Batts 3 and 4, On, and I'll give you the details if you feel like you want them.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We got Batts 1 and 2; Off, 3 and 4 are on; and Luny Batt is on the LMP Bus.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, Ed, I don't see the Luny Batt carrying much of a load here.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I got some (sample container) weights for you (as per Surface 5-4).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: The SRC number 2...(Stops to listen) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, the SRC number 2 weighs 41 pounds (18.6 kg).
SCB-3, which is in sample containment bag number 3, weighs 30 pounds (13.6 kg).
SCB-1, which is in sample containment bag number 4, weighs 26 pounds (11.8 kg).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: SCB number 3, which is the containment bag 3, is 30 pounds.
SCB number 1, which is in containment bag 4, is 26 pounds.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Keep going like that, you may have to throw away "Mully".
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: John's coming out of his suit now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I gather Doc's worked on a metabolic assessment for us.
Would you let us know in a little while?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, come at me again on that, please.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're just curious (about) the metabolic rates today.
Could you have the Doc work on that for us, and let us know in a little while.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: On John, the average ran about 785 (BTU/hr) average; and the LMP, on you, Charlie, ran about 870.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, we had predicted, on both of you, 890, so (you're) running to the good there (meaning that they are using less oxygen and cooling water than predicted).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Orion, the Docs tell me you were running about an 88 average heartbeat today.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'm out of my suit, and Charlie's getting out of his suit now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I understand that John's out of his suit and Charlie's coming out now, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Could you give him a call and verify that he's on left VHF antenna.
We're not receiving the VHF downlink on the bistatic radar.
(Pause) And come up on Transmitter A - Simplex - (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Houston, you said VHF A to Transmit, B to Receive, right?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Pause) Houston, what do you want me to tell him, again?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Casper, this is Orion transmitting in the blind.
Houston wants to verify that you are on the left antenna.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We kind of suspect he's off the headset since he is maintaining radio silence this pass for the bistatic (radar experiment).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Thank you very much - that's enough - (for) giving it a try and reconfigure your VHF back to a nominal configuration.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: But I believe he's off the headset.
If he was told to maintain radio silence, he don't hear.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're all convinced you're right, John.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Charlie's almost out of his suit now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You know, Houston, it would sure be handy to have something like a false floor to lay down in this thing, because we sure can't stand on the floor right now (because of all the dust).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You'd like a bath mat, eh, Charlie?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're ready for the lift-off times for revs 40 to 45.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're ready to go when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, you can give me the revs 40 to 45 (times) until Charlie gets on comm.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We've got T-41, 154 plus 02 plus 12; T-42, 156 plus 00 plus 41; T-43, 157 plus 59 plus 13; T-44, 159 plus 57 plus 44; T-45, 161 plus 56 plus 16; and that's it.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, readback: 41, 154:02:12; then 156:00 plus 41; 157 plus 59 plus 13; 159 plus 57 plus 44; 161 plus 56 plus 16.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What ground-elapsed time (GET) do you have now, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Right, and I can give you some times in your Surface Checklist for the different periods if you want them.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We don't have any idea what time it is.
Not that it makes any difference.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, John, I didn't catch that.
Do you want these times for page 5-5?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The EVA debriefing should start at 151 plus 55.
PLSS O2 and H2O recharge, 152 plus 55.
Okay, on 5-6, the midcourse conference - or MCC conference, correction - is 153 plus 25.
And rest period begins at 154 plus 35.
That's on the bottom of 5-7, and that goes for 8 hours.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, say again when the rest period...Where it goes.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, the rest period at the bottom of 5-7 is 154 plus 35 and it's for 8 hours.
And we're gonna try to get you to bed even early.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, you can't believe how dirty it is in this lunar module.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, a couple of bits of information we'd like from you before we start the debriefing.
We'd like to know if you drank all of the
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Did you drink any water when you got back in...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: There's no way to tell it unless you keep count of the swallows and we didn't do that (garbled).
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we'd like to remind you that you have some ointment in your medical kit if your fingers are sore there.
And just to make you feel warm, the next hand-off, we go to the 210-foot dish and we'll have High Bit Rate and Data for all your entire sleep period.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: When you're ready, we'd like to go on with...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: On the water, we were reconsidering the dosage.
It went down like a cool one (meaning a cold beer) after a geology trip, if you know what I mean.
There was about the same amount (or about 12 liquid ounces or about 360 ml).
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Discussing Station 4 here, we'd like your general impression of the rocks.
You described mostly breccias and white crystalline rocks.
Were there any others; and could you just talk about them a little bit?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Well, if we'd seen any that we recognized, we'd have told you about it.
I think the big blocks were just big brothers to the littler blocks.
That secondary that we were working up there was a classic.
And the pan will show that.
Must have come from South Ray.
It had a great block land in the middle, and then the fractures...ran off into all kinds of little blocks.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think that's about the only rock that we saw.
You know, we're not really sure.
It's our first guess as to what those rocks are up there: whether they're breccias or crystalline rocks.
I'm sure you all know that.
But most of them were very dust covered.
I only saw one clear crystalline in the whole EVA - except for the ones I chipped away - and that was the one I picked up.
I think it was Station 5, or 6.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think, Tony, my view is the same.
We had only a predominance of South Ray ejecta all around.
Mostly some of the smaller rakes (meaning rocks collected in rake samples) were given to Descartes.

It just occurred to me that everywhere we were, there was a boulder field of varying sizes and intensity and you could see eruptive force - you could look back at South Ray - and you could see the rays coming our way and spreading and fanning out as they flow.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know, it just may be that that South Ray Crater is so recent it's mantled everything - or most everything - around here.
You know, like we said about the rays at South Ray, some were white and some were black rays and just a little layer of dust.
I didn't see hardly a clear surface all day long.
I felt right at home like we were back in the United States studying geology and everybody said the rocks would be crystal clear.
Well, here it's all covered up.
And it is my opinion when you hit one and got a cleavage surface, that made all the difference in the world to a rock description.
But a person may not be able to do that (meaning breaking a rock to get a fresh surface) often.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Does that answer your question, Tony?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And the Station 4, 5, and 6 area, you mentioned that (at) 5 and 6 the surfaces got progressively firmer.
I wonder, could you see any contact or was it a gradual thing?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Just when we got off the Rover and you felt it under your feet.
The softest spot was up on the top, at Station 4.
Progressively firmer as we went out, like you say, went down to 6.
And you just noticed it when you got out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Of course, we were working in a couple of craters on Station 4.
Right on the - you know - right on the rim of the craters, where you'd expect it'd be softer, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Station 4, 5, and 6 also, we're pretty confident that the rocks at Station 4 were from South Ray.
At 5 and 6 in particular, though, was there any indication that the craters - either by their orientation or from the secondary blocks lying around - that the source was, in fact, South Ray?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, I would think the crater at 5 was a South Ray crater.
Although the rocks generally looked more rounded, maybe from a different layer (at the South Ray site).
(Garbled) pattern, you should be able to see that in the film, but the pattern of rocks were all in the far wall, which was away from South Ray Crater.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Incidentally, the Backroom is really impressed.
They are very excited about your choice of sampling on that inner wall of that crater - I guess it was at 5 or 6 - and think that you had a good chance there of getting real Descartes.
In fact, they think they've got it in the box.
What they're thinking right now is that the areas where you're able to kick up and see white underneath are (South) ray materials.
But you didn't see that (white underlayer) at 5 and 6.
So, we think you may have gotten Descartes there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: If this place had air, it'd sure be beautiful.
It's beautiful with or without air, but the scenery today up on top of Stone Mountain you'd have to be there to see and to believe it.
And I hope it showed up good on television...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, we have a bunch of questions we'd like to ask.
But we're going to knock it off and let you get some sleep and we'll try to pick your mind when you get home here.
But there's one thing on the Rover there.
On the drive from Station 10 back to the LM, did you notice that the Nav system worked?
Whether you got any distance (reading) on there in particular?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, we only came about 25 yards (garbled) I didn't notice, though, to tell you the truth.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did the numbers go to zero when you reset at Station 10?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I tell you, that beauty is holding up like a real jewel.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we've lost one piece of...
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I emptied that in the SRC and it's on the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I understand you dumped it into the SRC and the bag's on the Rover, still.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's just like we did in training.
You pack those SRCs and I could never...I mean those SCBs (into the SRC) - and I could never get the SRC closed.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We started dumping the stuff...(Stops to listen)
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We were wondering if...(correcting himself) wondering what happened to the (unused) SESC that was in the pocket there, but it looks like we're in good shape.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The SESC is back on the Rover.
I was looking at it just before we got in.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Why don't you head on with your meal and we'll have your Flight Plan updates for the (MCC)-conference period.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And what our plan is: we've got a lot of Flight Plan changes here - about seven pages worth - and we'll put them in your conference period and if we don't get them all up tonight, we'll send you to bed on time and we'll sneak them in the morning.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tell everybody in the Backroom "thank you" and we really enjoyed it today.
By golly, that view from up on Stone Mountain is something else.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm sorry, John, our comm's pretty bad.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No, I won't say it again.
I just wanted to say thank you to the Backroom boys.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, I'd like to say the same thing.
"Thanks." I think they did a great job and they kept us thinking and on our toes and came up with the right suggestions at the opportune times and thank you, too, for keeping us going.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hey, I went back in the Backroom after the EVA and they're just ecstatic back there.
I know we didn't see exactly when we expected to see (meaning evidence of volcanics), but we think you got everything that we went up there for.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That Rover really...If you could have seen some of the things that that Rover did today, you wouldn't believe it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You just can't believe the ridges and valleys and rilles, here.
I tell you, the local slope might be 2 or 3 degrees, but, man, that Double Spot, whoever picked that place to land, it's the only level spot around here.
Any place else, you'd really be in trouble.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, in about 50 minutes, we're going to get the 210 here, and then we'll have pretty good comm, so why don't you go eat now and do whatever you want, and we'll give you a call when we get the 210 and see if you're ready for the (checklist) updates.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Tony, (as per Surface 5-5) we'd like to start on that PLSS O2 recharge, topoff-type thing if it's been an hour (since the first oxygen charge).
Could y'all confirm that for us.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, I didn't understand your question.
You say you'd like to do it in an hour?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: No...No, the checklist says, "Connect LM O2 to PLSS; Hi PLSS O2 fill, open, then close." So, we're supposed to verify that 1 hour has elapsed since initial recharge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: You know, when we first got back in and we plugged up the oxygen to the PLSS, we're supposed to...From then to now, if it's been an hour, we can go ahead with this other topoff.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: It's 2 hours since you started the original charge.
If your question is, can you go right ahead with the topoff, the answer is "yes".
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Sorry; the problem here (is that) our comm is really bad.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Go Normal Voice and Hi Bit Rate.
(Brief static; Long Pause; Comm improves dramatically)
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Tony, you ought to have it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: (Pause) I can almost understand what you're saying, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Yeah, get the grits out of my mouth.
Is that our friends in Australia tonight?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hello, our friends out on the Mojave.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And why don't you give us a call when you're ready to take these Flight Plan updates?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Houston, we're going off biomed for a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, we'd like to try to get the bulk of this Flight Plan update up tonight because we won't have the 210-foot dish until 2 hours before lift-off.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We'll be with you as quick as we can.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: No, I don't think you do; but it's all right.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We've got both PLSSs recharged with water and the ten-minute oxygen topoff.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What's your best guess about where we landed?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're just going by what you said.
Originally, we had you, I think, 150 meters north and 200 west and you said, today, that you were just north (of the LM).

We thought that the first estimate of 150 north and 200 west was compatible with the LRV data that you've got, the nav data, we kind of liked that one, but I haven't seen anything from the planners yet.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: From where we were at Station 4 up at Stone Mountain, it looked like...I could see Double Spot and maybe we're just a little past it (to the west), but not much.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Much as I'd like to sit here and talk about the landing site, Deke's here looking over my shoulder, and he's telling us we've got to get on with this Flight Plan update.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Let me get Charlie on comm.
I want him to hear it, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, in your Surface Checklist, page 5-7.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, change: "Stow hammocks, roll up with sleep restraints" to - that's on the right-hand column about three or four lines down - change it to "Stow hammocks and sleep restraints in
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: (Amused) We could probably have figured that out.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, on that same column, at the bottom of the page, says "Lift-off time data in book for rev 37 to 43," we'll change that to "46 to 51".
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, and right under that on the "Pro Verb 37:06 Enter," we'd like to delete both of those lines.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: On the left-hand column, it says "Empty ETB as follows." We'd like to delete "1 - HCEX MAG D and LCG compartment."
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, and after "Stow in ETB" at the top of the next column, we'd like to add "1 - HCEX MAG D." (Long Pause)
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, that's D as in Delta.
And then in the same column down, it says "2 - 16 Mags R and S." We'd like to change that to "1 - 16 Mag S."
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, that's S as in Sugar.
Okay, on 5-9, left-hand column near the bottom of the page, it says "Stow ICG in ICG bag." (Pause) We'd like to change that to "Stow ICG (empty pockets) in
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And then at the bottom of the page after the "Used food in containers," we'd like to add two lines.
The first one is "LCG adapters," (pause) and the second line is "Urine receptacle."
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, "LCG adapters, two; and urine receptacle, one."
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: On 5-10 (pause) delete all the page, and add: "Battery management at 164 plus 45" in the right-hand column.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, "Battery management at 164 hours and 45 minutes."
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, I'll send up the stuff for your cue cards later.
(Pause) And I'd like to know if you'd like the change in times in the Surface Checklist.
That's what some of these changes are.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, I won't send those, then.
(Pause) Okay, we'll have to go on to 7-5, then.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, on 7-5, delete "Battery management", "On Houston cue: Telemetry PCM, Lo", "S-Band, Voice and Down Voice Backup".

tokens: (Pause) And we have some questions on the
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: They keep piling them in front of me.
Okay, on the broken CDR's OPS antenna, we'd like you to: one, remove any of the sharp edges with the scissors and examine the entire length of the antenna for cracks.
And we'll give you time to dig that out.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The reason that it's broke is on account of I climbed in here with it open, because we're being rushed there toward the end.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm sorry, I shouldn't have let you get so far behind.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We had plenty of consumables, and where's the fire?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: What we'd like you to do, as best you can, a patchwork job on that antenna.
We saw about a 15 dB loss from you when it broke.
And we'll probably ask you and Charlie to switch OPSs tomorrow, because you're relaying.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We'll give you some words on that in the morning.
But, if you see any cracks, we'd like you to tape around the antenna about three loops, it says, such that it covers the crack of about a half inch on either side.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: The antenna is about a half inch longer than the pen-lights that we carry, and it's in good shape except for a little crack at the top where it broke off.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That crack on the end there, we'd just like you to trim that back.

transcript.speaker: England

tokens: We already did that, and it's not cracked down there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Just right up at the end.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And Charlie's got three loops of tape around it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: As far as we're concerned, the Government's gonna allow you to sleep.

transcript.speaker: England

tokens: First we've heard about it, but we'll do it, I guess.
Tony, could we beg off on a PRD readings unless (lost under Tony but probably something like 'really necessary'.)
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got the suits piled one on top of the other, and it'll be a mess.
The bags are up around the legs.
It'll be a mess to dig them out of there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Deke's standing here, and he said y'all did a beautiful day's work, and he's anxious to see y'all hit the sack ASAP (meaning "as soon as possible").
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And, I sure agree on that beautiful day's work.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Where's this MCC (Mission Control Center)-Houston...(Stops to listen) Where's this MCC-H conference at?

tokens: Oh, I thought maybe you guys were going to the Wheel or something.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: (Laughs) You mean you didn't enjoy it (meaning the MCC-H conference)?
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I would be interested in what our EVA is gonna look like for tomorrow.
Maybe I was off comm and you told Charlie or something.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We haven't said anything about it.
The planners will be back working on it.
I can tell you what it looked like before EVA-2.
It was a 5-hour EVA with about an hour up on North Ray and about 20 minutes at Station 13 and essentially the rest of the time back in the LM/ALSEP area, especially east of the LM, there.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: It's probably subject to a change, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: You got a lot of good data today, and that'll make them think about it overnight, anyway.
But I'm sure everybody's sort of drooling about North Ray.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: I kind of feel like if we're ever going to be able to sort this out, North Ray's probably the place.
Personally, to be able to get down 200 meters is something we probably ought to do to see just how complicated this thing really is.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: But let's not think geology, let's go to bed.
Incidentally, we'd like to go to Low Bit Rate and Down Voice Backup.
I guess with a 210 we can't assure being hooked up.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: Okay, Lo Bit Rate and Down Voice Backup.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Tony, whose arrhythmias y'all want to look at tonight?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We still put in a strong vote for North Ray.
It looks accessible, and there's a tremendous block up there we'd like to look at.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We heard you talk about that.
I'm gonna go back and talk to them before I go home.
We'll see y'all in the morning.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: That was a good day's work for yourself.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We've been talking about that crater that we took a pan of as we spun around and...I said, looked like Big Sag.
John and I are leaning towards endogenic on that, and hopefully from the films that we got you might be able to sort that out.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We'll sure take that in consideration.
I don't know whether it'll affect tomorrow, but I think it's a good observation.
(Pause) And, Charlie, we still have a Hi bit rate.
We'd like to go to Lo.
transcript.speaker: England

tokens: And Charlie's changing the (ECS) LiOH canister right now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If you're reading me, you have a lot of static on the up-link.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Sorry about that, and we had the up-link turned down.
Now I guess somebody...Did you hear us comment?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Somebody was blasting us out of the cockpit.
I turned the Squelch, Off, but now whoever...Who's that talking, is that Stu?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Boy, you're sure looking good up there.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Greatest thrill of my whole life.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, we'll do something about that uplink, and we're working maybe the switch configuration now.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, this is beautiful right now, Stu.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: There would be an old saying that would apply to that, then, wouldn't there?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hey, Charlie, could we have you check your uplink Squelch.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're going to kill the uplink.
If you hear the noise, give us a call back.
And have a good night's rest, boy; we'll have a cool one (meaning a cold beer) for you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hey, we haven't gotten to the pre-sleep checklist part.
If it calls for Squelch, Enable, I can turn it on, and we'll be okay.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Looks like we wanted you to go to sleep a little early, and we got ahead of you.
Could you call us just when you finish your pre-sleep checklist, and at that time we'll have the right configuration.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, hey, since we only got a watch on Houston time, could you give us a time...what time we're supposed to be up in the morning?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're going to wake you up 8 hours from now.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, but we're about a half hour away from being asleep.
(Pause) We're still gonna get up at 08:35, right?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We'll give you a call at 7 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: (Long Pause) Hey, only with the understanding that that doesn't take a half hour off our EVA, huh?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Flight says that since they got all those good up-links changes to you this evening, everything looks real clean.
And he feels that you can get into the EVA on time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa
